video_description: Ficha pública do ASIM no European Film Gateway, com acesso mediado por EFG/Mémoire Filmique e player quando detectado. | título alternativo: One time, the lime makers (part 1) | gênero: Amateur film | duração: 00:11:23 | coleção: Films Gabriel Mayans | direção: Gabriel Mayans | cor: Colour | som: With sound | direitos: In Copyright / Gabriel Mayans | fonte de mídia: https://vimeo.com/149314977 | ver também: https://memoirefilmiquedusud.eu/idurl/1/1882 | Documentary filmed in Felanitx (Mallorca) in Pedro Escribano's lime kiln. We are shown how to make a lime kiln, the preparation of limestone and how the blocks are placed in rows inside the oven alongside pieces of timber that fuel the fire and separate the different layers of blocks. Finally, the oven is closed with stones, forming a kind of vault called fig bread, which is covered with a thin layer of fused lime so the fire does not escape between the interstices.

video_description: Ficha pública do ASIM no European Film Gateway, com acesso mediado por EFG/Mémoire Filmique e player quando detectado. | título alternativo: One time, the weavers | gênero: Amateur film | duração: 00:11:14 | coleção: Films Gabriel Mayans | direção: Gabriel Mayans | cor: Colour | som: With sound | direitos: In Copyright / Gabriel Mayans | fonte de mídia: https://vimeo.com/149315511 | ver também: https://memoirefilmiquedusud.eu/idurl/1/1888 | Documentary filmed in Can Bujosa in Santa Maria del Camí (Mallorca). Mestre Guillem Bujosa Rossello is the oldest weaving mestre ("master") in Mallorca. He recites some verses while working and shows us the various tools of his trade, including the shuttles and the spinning wheel. His son Guillem Bujosa shows a fabric press from the 16th century. We see the patrons of the weavers' guild. Mestre William makes a demonstration of how the loom works while we are given a series of biographical and historical notes about his workshop. Finally, we can see some different types of fabrics and artworks.

We can see how sheep are milked while a tune is heard. Cheese making starts by putting the fogasser (a white piece of cloth) over the opening of a pitcher to strain the milk. Thistle flowers are chopped and are also added in the fogasser. The milk is stirred well and put on the fire for two hours. A fogasser is placed in a basin, the cheese is put on top and the excess of milk is removed. The corners of the fogasser are stretched and tied together. The pieces of cheese are pressed to remove the milk they still contain for 2 or 3 hours. The milk that is left is put on the fire to get the brossat or mato cheeses. video_description: Ficha pública do ASIM no European Film Gateway, com acesso mediado por EFG/Mémoire Filmique e player quando detectado. | título alternativo: Sandstone time (part 1) | gênero: Amateur film | duração: 00:10:19 | coleção: Films Gabriel Mayans | direção: Gabriel Mayans | cor: Colour | som: With sound | direitos: In Copyright / Gabriel Mayans | fonte de mídia: https://vimeo.com/149315701 | ver também: https://memoirefilmiquedusud.eu/idurl/1/1889 | Documentary on the sandstone quarries in Campos (Mallorca). Tomeu "Xalapot" makes a demonstration of how limestone was extracted with ancient techniques. video_description: Ficha pública do ASIM no European Film Gateway, com acesso mediado por EFG/Mémoire Filmique e player quando detectado. | título alternativo: Sandstone time (part 2) | gênero: Amateur film | duração: 00:09:52 | coleção: Films Gabriel Mayans | direção: Gabriel Mayans | cor: Colour | som: With sound | direitos: In Copyright / Gabriel Mayans | fonte de mídia: https://vimeo.com/149315822 | ver também: https://memoirefilmiquedusud.eu/idurl/1/1890 | Documentary on the sandstone quarries in Campos (Mallorca). We see the modern quarries of Tomeu in the Son Toni Amer stately home, which uses modern machinery and methods. We can see how the daily activity in a modern open-cast mine is carried out and how the ground is cleared for its expansion. Next, we are shown the work in Son Oliver, where the rocks are mined underground. We see how underground working methods and how ancient techniques are employed alongside modern ones. The small bits of sandstone are used for the manufacture of fine gravel.

The caves extend to a depth of 25 m, reaching 2.4 km in length. The Drac is a system consisting of four caves, called Black Cave, White Cave, Cave of Lluis Salvador, and Cave of the French, which are connected to each other. The caves were formed by the entry of water from the Mediterranean Sea, and some scholars consider their formation could date back from the Miocene. video_description: Ficha pública do ASIM no European Film Gateway, com acesso mediado por EFG/Mémoire Filmique e player quando detectado. | título alternativo: The Nights of Count Mal | gênero: Advertising film | duração: 00:04:04 | coleção: Films Foment del Turisme de Mallorca | produtora: Izy Publicidad | cor: Colour | som: With sound | direitos: In Copyright / Foment del Turisme de Mallorca | fonte de mídia: https://vimeo.com/148596654 | ver também: https://memoirefilmiquedusud.eu/idurl/1/1869 | Brief advertising report with a view to promote the Comte Mal Restaurant, located in the former estate of Son Termens, in the municipality of Bunyola. We can see a folklore group with traditional costumes, playing music and dancing folk dances in the courtyard of the estate. In this restaurant, very popular, tourists are greeted by the feudal lord and his court and, while having traditional dishes of the Mallorcan cuisine for dinner, enjoy the show of medieval jousting, where some knights show their prowess with arms. Both dinner and show are chaired by Comte Mal, historical figure who lived in 17th-century Mallorca, and gave rise to numerous traditional legends.

Images of Palma's old town: the parish church of Santa Creu, the seaside neighborhood of Puig de Sant Pere, Montenegro Street, Quarterera Square, Mar Street, Can Formiguera Street and Almudaina street. Next, we see various stately homes: Can Berga, the Solleric Palace, Can Olesa, Can Vivot and Can Oleo as well as the interiors of some them. Footage of remarkable buildings, such as the Fish Market, the Casa de l'Almoina, the Consolat de Mar, the cloister of Sant Antoniet and the Town Hall of Palma. Lastly, we are shown some aspects of the modern town: Palau de la Diputacio, Teatre Principal, Colom Street, Institut Ramon Llull and Espanya Square.

video_description: Ficha pública do ASIM no European Film Gateway, com acesso mediado por EFG/Mémoire Filmique e player quando detectado. | título alternativo: The Feast of the Pedal | gênero: Documentary film | duração: 00:11:46 | produtora: Veloz Sport Balear | cor: B/W / Tinted | som: Without sound | direitos: Public Domain | fonte de mídia: https://vimeo.com/140638765 | ver também: https://memoirefilmiquedusud.eu/idurl/1/1677 | Documentary about a party held by the Veloç Sport Balear, a sports society founded in 1896 in Palma to promote cycling. The Pedal Festival took place in Son Company (Montuiri) in 1915 and in Arenal in 1917; footage of the start of the race from Espanya Square in Palma, the cyclists riding idly through the streets, arrival in Arenal and the cyclists on the road. We see different participants, curious scenes and the arrival of the authorities. Next, we are shown the masses in the church of Our Lady of Breastfeeding in Arenal and Son Company (Montuiri). We are shown a wide shot of the Arenal, racing on the beach and some folk dance. Next, we see people in Son Company enjoying lunch and folk dances; lastly the return of the cyclists to Palma.
